name: flock-watchdog-sites
title: 5 Key Flock Watchdog Sites
description: Public civic-tech resources for ALPR / Flock Safety transparency. Reference links only — not operational surveillance. Colorado dashboard context: institutional awareness of fixed ALPR deployments and public contracts.

ethics.note: These sites aggregate public records, FOIA/CORA templates, and crowdsourced camera maps. Do not use this panel to track individuals. Professional / institutional awareness only.

sites.name: Alpr.wtf
sites.summary: Directory of U.S. jurisdictions tied to Flock/ALPR networks, with ready-to-file public-records request templates.

sites.name: EyesOnFlock.com
sites.summary: Crowdsourced and transparency-portal data on Flock cameras, city contracts, and agency usage patterns.

sites.name: Deflock.me
sites.summary: Nationwide open-source ALPR map tracking Flock and other LPR deployments, removals, and community resistance.
sites.note: Also published at deflock.org; map counts change frequently.

sites.name: HaveIBeenFlocked.com
sites.summary: Search public Flock audit logs and agency reports to see whether a plate appears in disclosed lookup records.

sites.name: DontGetFlocked.com
sites.summary: Route planner that counts ALPR/Flock cameras along a path and suggests lower-surveillance alternatives (FlockHopper).

colorado_note: For Colorado, prefer CORA requests via Alpr.wtf agency templates and confirm local contracts via city/county meeting minutes and EyesOnFlock. Denver metro remains P0 for institutional awareness only.
